issue.title: Nginx 405 Method Not Allowed Error for POST Requests to /api/form Endpoint
issue.description: Nginx is returning a 405 'Method Not Allowed' error for POST requests to the /api/form endpoint, even though the location block is configured to proxy requests to a Node.js server running on port 4321.
symptoms: GET requests to /api/form work correctly and return a JSON response from the Node.js server
POST requests to /api/form return a 405 'Method Not Allowed' error from nginx
The issue persists even after restarting nginx and verifying the configuration
Node.js server is confirmed to be running and accepting connections on port 4321

task.objective: Fix the nginx configuration to properly handle POST requests to the /api/form endpoint
steps_taken: Verified Node.js server is running correctly on port 4321
Confirmed GET requests work properly through the proxy
Checked nginx configuration syntax - no errors found
Restarted nginx service to ensure configuration is loaded
Modified location block pattern to use regex
Added explicit method allowances to the location block
remaining_actions: Further investigate why nginx is returning 405 errors for POST requests
Check for any hidden nginx modules or configurations that might be blocking POST requests
Consider alternative approaches like using a different port or endpoint path
Review nginx error logs for more detailed information about the 405 error

warnings: SSL stapling warnings about OCSP responder not found
Conflicting server name warnings for zerodolg.ru and www.zerodolg.ru
